$comment: Normative appendix for L1-TB-01; extracted from hooks/shared/teachback_schema.py at plugin 4.6.8 / commit b4041ccf (required-field tuple, variety-acknowledgment value set, reasoning-reconstruction sub-key set).
title: Teachback payload
description: A Specialist's pre-work restatement of its dispatched mission, recorded in the gate record's metadata. Shape-only: the acceptance/rejection lifecycle lives in the prose requirements L1-TB-01 through L1-TB-06.

understanding.description: What the Specialist understands it is building: key constraints and interfaces.

most_likely_wrong.description: The part of the restated understanding the Specialist is least confident about.

least_confident_item.description: One specific assumption the Specialist asks the Lead to confirm.

first_action.description: The first concrete step the Specialist will take after acceptance.

variety_acknowledgment.description: The Specialist's review of the dispatch's variety rationales. An object, never a free-text string.

concern.description: Names the smell; required whenever the acknowledgment value is not "yes".

reasoning_reconstruction.description: Method-level reconstruction of the upstream actor's reasoning. Optional below the declared variety band floor and required at or above it (L1-TB-06). Exactly these three sub-keys, each a non-empty, non-whitespace string.

examples.understanding: Implement the refund worker behind the existing payments API, idempotent under queue retries, without touching the ledger service's write path.
examples.most_likely_wrong: Whether multi-currency partial refunds round at the worker or at the ledger.
examples.least_confident_item: That the ledger accepts reversal entries with the original charge's currency.
examples.first_action: Read the ledger service's reversal-entry contract before writing the worker interface.

reasoning_reconstruction.decision_attribution: The designer chose queue-driven retries because the payment provider's API times out under load, making synchronous refunds unreliable.
reasoning_reconstruction.assumption_trace: This depends on the queue delivering at-least-once, and on the idempotency key being stable across retries.
reasoning_reconstruction.contingency_clause: If the queue were exactly-once, the idempotency requirement could relax to a plain uniqueness check.
